meaning: n.半位元组,细咬,轻咬,啃v.一点一点地咬,细咬,吹毛求疵
examples: A soft voice cried from the parlour: "Nibble, nibble, gnaw, who is nibbling at my little house?"
This is strange because the sorts of lab animals the researchers looked attend to be given lots of food and left to nibble at leisure.
The birds nibble at the brickwork.
"Twenty-five calories is not a serving, so we nibble without thinking we are eating, " he says.
The third quarter was scrappy but Westmeath gradually began to nibble at Derry's lead.
